option.name: text
option.value: * Provide all code examples in Kotlin.

option.name: name
option.value: 01.01 setup .aiexclude

option.name: text
option.value: Create an .aiexclude file in the root of the project. Include build directories, local.properties and a wildcard for .keystore files. Add an exclude for any secrets.xml files in any of the directories.

option.name: name
option.value: 01.02 AGENTS.md

option.name: text
option.value: Setup an AGENTS.md file for this project. Include that we should use Compose, Navigation 3, Material 3 and architecture as recommended on https://developer.android.com/topic/architecture/recommendations

option.name: name
option.value: 02.01 fix crash backup if button doesn't work

option.name: text
option.value: I'm getting the following error in my running application. The error is: FATAL EXCEPTION: main ``` FATAL EXCEPTION: main Process: net.maiatoday.moodsnap, PID: 5579 java.lang.IllegalStateException: The query result was empty, but expected a single row to return a NON-NULL object of type 'net.maiatoday.moodsnap.`data`.MoodEntry'. at net.maiatoday.moodsnap.data.MoodEntryDao_Impl.getEntryById$lambda$0(MoodEntryDao_Impl.kt:217) at net.maiatoday.moodsnap.data.MoodEntryDao_Impl$$ExternalSyntheticLambda0.invoke(D8$$SyntheticClass:0) with tag AndroidRuntime ``` Fix this issue

option.name: name
option.value: 02.02 Investigate mood id for new and updated moods

option.name: text
option.value: Look at the @MoodEntryDao.kt , @MoodEntry.kt and the @AddEditViewModel.kt What I am seeing is that the id is not updated for a new entry and the id is always -1 even though this is an autoGenerated primary key. Why could this be? Suggest some fixes. I can either edit an existing entry or create a new one. Both should work

option.name: name
option.value: 02.03 improved update and insert

option.name: text
option.value: Look at the @AddEditViewModel.kt . In the init function I want you to determine if this is a new or an update scenario. Then set a local private value `isNew` to true if it is a new mood. Then set another local variable moodId that is non-nullable to 0 if it is a new mood or set it to the entryId if it is an edit. When the mood is saved use the moodId and the flag to either update or insert. moodId will then always have the correct value.

option.name: name
option.value: 03.00 Architecture improvements

option.name: text
option.value: Refactor the data layer to follow Android Architecture guidelines by introducing a Repository and a UseCase. Do not modify the UI layout code (HomeScreen.kt). 1.Create Repository: ◦In net.maiatoday.moodsnap.data, create an interface MoodRepository. ◦In the same package, create an implementation OfflineMoodRepository that takes MoodEntryDao as a constructor dependency. ◦ Expose methods in the repository that mirror the DAO signatures exactly: getAllEntries(): Flow<List<MoodEntry>>, getEntryById, insert, delete, and update. 2.Dependency Injection: ◦In net.maiatoday.moodsnap.di, create a new file RepositoryModule.kt. ◦Create an abstract class or interface annotated with @Module and @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class). ◦Use @Binds to bind MoodRepository to OfflineMoodRepository. 3.Create UseCase: ◦Create a new package net.maiatoday.moodsnap.domain. ◦Create GetMoodEntriesUseCase. ◦It should inject MoodRepository and invoke getAllEntries(). 4.Update ViewModel: ◦Refactor HomeViewModel to inject GetMoodEntriesUseCase instead of MoodEntryDao. ◦Update the entries flow to use this use case.

option.name: name
option.value: 03.01 Edit Mood screen

option.name: text
option.value: Refactor AddEditScreen.kt using Jetpack Compose and Material3. The screen must observe AddEditViewModel's uiState. Use @/.agents/resources/03-ui-scaffold/AddEditScreen.jpg and look at @AddEditScreen.kt @NavGraph.kt @AddEditViewModel.kt 1. Scaffold & Navigation: Wrap the screen in a Scaffold. Add a TopAppBar with a navigation icon (ArrowBack) that triggers a lambda onBackClick: () -> Unit. 2. Boolean Toggles (Habits): For the movement, sleep, and sunlight boolean fields, create a reusable composable row. •Visuals: Show the label (e.g., "Movement") on the left. On the right, show a 'Thumbs Up' icon and a 'Thumbs Down' icon. •Logic: Since the data is Boolean: ◦If true, tint 'Thumbs Up' primary color. ◦If false, tint 'Thumbs Down' error/primary color (or grey out 'Thumbs Up'). 'Thumbs Down' means 'No' 3. Mood Score (Emojis): Replace standard icons with a row of emojis to represent the moodScore (Int). •Map 1..5 to: , , , , . •Highlight the selected emoji based on state.moodScore. 4. Tags Section: Display state.tags in a FlowRow (or LazyRow). Add an FilledTonalButton or IconButton with a '+' icon next to them. It should be clickable but do nothing for now. 5. Energy Bar: Add the energy bar section similar to the existing implementation. 6. Create a preview of the composable screen Architecture: Ensure strict State Hoisting. The AddEditScreen composable should take state: AddEditUiState and event lambdas (e.g., onMoodChange: (Int) -> Unit) as parameters, rather than passing the ViewModel down."

option.name: name
option.value: 03.02 Home to detail screen

option.name: text
option.value: Refactor the HomeScreen to separate the current list content into a new screen, but keep the HomeViewModel attached to HomeScreen for future implementation. Use and look at @HomeScreen.kt @NavGraph.kt @HomeViewModel.kt 1.Create MoodHistoryScreen: ◦Create a new Composable named MoodHistoryScreen. ◦Move the LazyColumn and MoodEntryItem code here from HomeScreen. ◦Create a new ViewModel (e.g., HistoryViewModel) for this screen that duplicates the logic for fetching entries so the list functions correctly. 2.Update Navigation (Screen.kt): ◦Add a new object History to the Screen sealed class with route "history". 3.Update HomeScreen: ◦Top Bar: Add a Material 3 TopAppBar. Include a "Settings" icon (no action needed) and a "List" icon (use Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.List). ◦Navigation: When the "List" icon is clicked, navigate to the History route. ◦Content: Remove the list. Replace it with a centered text placeholder: "Home Dashboard". 4. Previews: create previews for all new screens ◦FAB: Keep the existing floating action button and its navigation logic. ◦ViewModel: Keep HomeViewModel injected into this screen.

option.name: name
option.value: 03.03 UI for Home Screen

option.name: text
option.value:
Refactor the HomeScreen to match the design in @/.agent/resources/03-ui-scaffold/HomeScreen.jpg.
Replace the existing Placeholder content with a UI that implements the following requirements. Ensure the implementation follows Clean Architecture (UI -> Domain -> Data).
# 1. UI Layer (Jetpack Compose) - **Top Bar:** "Mood Snap" title with a Menu icon (left) and Settings icon (right). - **Mood Graph:** A bar chart showing the daily mood score for the past 7 days. - **Current Status (Right of/Next to graph):** - A large visual icon representing the current mood. - **Averages (Below graph):** - Text: "Past week: [Average Mood Score]" - Text: "Energy: [Average Energy Score]" (displayed below the mood average). - **Tags Section:** - Display tags used in the last 7 days (e.g., "Stress", "Junk Food"). - **Layout:** Use a **horizontal scrolling row** (e.g., `LazyRow`) for the tags so they don't consume too much vertical space. - **Habit Tracker:** Three rows (Movement, Sunlight, Sleep). - Each row displays 7 stars total. - The number of **filled** stars should equal the number of days in the past week where that habit was marked as true. - **FAB:** - Keep the existing Floating Action Button in the bottom-right. - Retain its current icon (+) and `onClick` functionality (navigation). - **Preview:** Create a Compose Preview populated with dummy data matching the sketch.
# 2. Domain Layer - Create/Update a UseCase (e.g., `GetWeeklySummaryUseCase`). - **Logic:** - **Mood Average:** Calculate average mood over the last 7 days (ignore days with no entry). - **Energy Average:** Calculate average energy over the last 7 days (ignore days with no entry). - **Habit Counts:** Count total days in the past 7 days where `movement`, `sunlight`, and `sleep` were true. - **Tags:** Aggregate unique tags used in the past 7 days.
# 3. Data Layer - Ensure the Repository exposes a Flow returning entries for the last 7 days.

option.name: name
option.value: 03.chat better prompts for bug fixing

option.name: text
option.value: Here is a prompt I want to improve. It concerns the @HomeScreen.kt . Help me make it clearer so the agent will perform at its best. Here is the prompt On the HomeScreen do the following fixes: 1. Remove the hamburger menu icon on the top bar of the HomeScreen. 2. Look at the scale of the vertical bars of the graph, there is something off for large values

option.name: name
option.value: 04.01 deeplink

option.name: text
option.value: is there some guidelines or tools in android studio to create a deeplink to the AddEdit screen

option.name: name
option.value: 04.02 tags exploration

option.name: text
option.value: Here is a prompt I need to improve: <prompt> The MoodEntry in this app has tags but there is no way to show which tags are in the entry on the UI. There is also no way to add new tags. What do you suggest I do to improve this. I want to be able to see all the tags that are added to an entry. I want to be able to remove a tag. I want to be able to easily add tags that I have already created. I was thinking about toggleable pill buttons but not sure about the UX. Give me a few scenarios of how to fix this. </prompt> evaluate this prompt and rewrite it for clarity. Don't implement anything. Add reference to any files as needed. Also if you think it will help break the process into a series of that can be executed sequentiall so I can have save points inbetween. Include a first step to get the database into an appropriate state for this feature. Feel free to create any new tables or entities if necessary.

option.name: name
option.value: 04.03 Cleanup Tags Column

option.name: text
option.value:
# Cleanup Task: Remove Deprecated `tags` Column from `MoodEntry`
## Context We have successfully refactored the tagging system to use a many-to-many relationship (`MoodEntryTagCrossRef`). The original `tags: List<String>` column in the `MoodEntry` table is now redundant and should be removed to keep the database schema clean and avoid confusion.
## Goal Remove the `tags` property from the `MoodEntry` entity and the corresponding column from the database.
## Steps
### 1. Update `MoodEntry` Entity * File: `app/src/main/java/net/maiatoday/moodsnap/data/MoodEntry.kt` * Action: Remove `val tags: List<String>`.
### 2. Update `MoodEntryDao` * File: `app/src/main/java/net/maiatoday/moodsnap/data/MoodEntryDao.kt` * Action: Ensure no queries or inserts reference the old `tags` column. (Most should be fine as Room handles the entity structure, but double-check custom queries if any).
### 3. Update Database Version & Migration * File: `app/src/main/java/net/maiatoday/moodsnap/data/AppDatabase.kt` * Action: * Increment `version` to 3. * Add a migration strategy. * *Option A (Development)*: `.fallbackToDestructiveMigration()` is already enabled, so this will automatically wipe and recreate the table without the column. * *Option B (Production)*: Write a `Migration` (2 -> 3) that: 1. Creates a new table `mood_entries_new` without the `tags` column. 2. Copies data from `mood_entries` to `mood_entries_new`. 3. Drops `mood_entries`. 4. Renames `mood_entries_new` to `mood_entries`.
### 4. Fix Compilation Errors * Files: * `AddEditViewModel.kt`: Update `MoodEntry` constructor calls to remove the `tags` argument. * `Converters.kt`: Remove unused converters for `List<String>` if they are no longer used elsewhere. * Any test files that instantiate `MoodEntry`.
## Verification * Build and run the app. * Verify that adding/viewing mood entries still works correctly with the new relationship-based tags.

option.name: name
option.value: 04.04 Fix HomeScreen tags

option.name: text
option.value: I see from the previous changes that `Updated GetWeeklySummaryUseCase: Temporarily returns empty tags (fix to compile).` could you fix this by creating the task getTagsForDataRange

option.name: name
option.value: 05.01 Research ResonanceEngine

option.name: text
option.value: Run the app in the emulator. I notice on the HomeScreen there is a new Resonance value. What is this value? How is it calculated? What does it mean for the user. Give me a tldr bullet point answer.

option.name: name
option.value: 05.02 ResonanceEngine detail

option.name: text
option.value: Give me more detail on what it actually means for a user

option.name: name
option.value: 05.03 Migrate unit tests

option.name: text
option.value:
Objective: Develop a comprehensive research-backed strategy to migrate the unit test suite from JUnit 4 to JUnit 5. The primary goal is to enable the use of JUnit 5's @ParameterizedTest for more robust testing of the ResonanceEngine.
Your plan must address the following:
1. Infrastructure & Dependencies: Identify the necessary updates for libs.versions.toml and app/build.gradle.kts. This includes adding the JUnit 5 Jupiter engine and evaluating the need for the android-junit5 Gradle plugin. 2. Lifecycle & Annotation Migration: Detail the mapping of JUnit 4 annotations (e.g., @Test, @Before, @After, @Ignore) to their JUnit 5 equivalents. 3. Handling Rules & Extensions: Analyze existing @Rule usage (e.g., InstantTaskExecutorRule or Hilt's HiltAndroidRule) and provide a strategy for migrating them to JUnit 5 @ExtendWith or equivalent extensions. Highlight any known compatibility issues with Hilt and JUnit 5 in an Android environment. 4. ResonanceEngine Expansion: Design a specific step to rewrite ResonanceEngineTest.kt using @ParameterizedTest. Outline the input/output sets (e.g., @ValueSource, @MethodSource) needed to cover edge cases, mood transitions, and tag interactions. 5. Audit of All Unit Tests: A phased approach to migrate all existing tests in app/src/test/ to ensure no regressions and consistent assertion styles (e.g., switching to org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions).
Constraints: Identify any technical risks before proposing the timeline. Provide the plan only; do not implement any changes or modify files.
